item.title: Supreme Court sides with man who challenged law barring drug users from having guns
item.description: The Supreme Court on Thursday ruled in favor of a Texas man who challenged a federal law that bars certain drug users from having firearms.

item.title: Watch Live: Obama Presidential Center opening ceremony
item.description: The Obama Presidential Center, museum and library opens in Chicago with a star-studded grand opening ceremony and public watch party on Midway Plaisance.

item.title: "Lady Gucci," who had her ex-husband killed, could soon inherit $23M fortune
item.description: Despite spending 18 years in prison for orchestrating the murder of her ex-husband, fashion heir Maurizio Gucci, Patrizia Reggiani may be about to inherit a fortune.

item.title: Obama Presidential Center opens in Chicago
item.description: The Obama Presidential Center, museum and library opens in Chicago with a star-studded grand opening ceremony and public watch party on Midway Plaisance.

item.title: Gas prices dip below $4 a gallon on average for first time in months after Iran deal progress
item.description: Gas prices have dropped in reaction to President Trump signing a memorandum of understanding with Iran -- but they're still a dollar higher than they were before the war, according to AAA data. CBS News business analyst Jill Schlesinger explains how markets are reacting to global relations.

item.title: Destructive tornado "sounded like thunder" as it hurled tree into Illinois home, witness says
item.description: At least seven tornadoes descended on parts of the Midwest and Southeast Wednesday night, ripping through homes and buildings and leaving a trail of destruction in their wake. CBS News' Rob Marciano reports.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic ramps up in wake of U.S.-Iran deal signing
item.description: More tankers are transiting the Strait of Hormuz after 111 days of gridlock, but Iran and the U.S. still have much to discuss.

item.title: Live Updates: Knicks celebrate championship win with parade in NYC
item.description: The New York Knicks are being honored with a ticker-tape parade Thursday through the Canyon of Heroes in Lower Manhattan.

item.title: Trump says negotiation deadline for Iran is flexible "as long as they're behaving"
item.description: President Trump signed a memorandum of understanding with Iran on Wednesday. Now, Iran and the U.S. will begin negotiating for 60 days. CBS News' Kathryn Watson and BBC News correspondent Jon Donnison have more on the contents of the deal. Also, Florida International University professor Eric Lob joins with more insight.

item.title: Facing bipartisan criticism of Iran deal, Trump lashes out at "fools"
item.description: Republican Senators Bill Cassidy, Thom Tillis and Ted Cruz have been critical of the 14-point memorandum of understanding signed on Wednesday.

item.title: U.S. plans expansion of denaturalization push, aiming for 250 cases by fall
item.description: The Trump administration expects to try to revoke the U.S. citizenship of more than 250 foreign-born citizens by October, a Justice Department official said.

item.title: Waymo recalls robotaxis after some vehicles entered construction zone
item.description: The recall follows multiple incidents in which Waymo robotaxis drove past ramp-closure signs and into freeway construction zones.

item.title: Can bankruptcy protect your retirement accounts from creditors?
item.description: Bankruptcy could shield many retirement assets, but the level of protection it offers depends on the account.

item.title: A NYC cab driver's taxi was trashed in Knicks celebration — then French Montana stepped in
item.description: New York City taxi driver Noureddine Bitat was violently assaulted and his yellow cab was smashed in the mayhem following the Knicks' Game 4 win. Grammy-nominated rapper French Montana shares how he joined others to help the cabbie get back on his feet.

item.title: How much interest can $50,000 earn now? Here are 4 account options to know.
item.description: Have $50,000 that you're looking to grow further? Here's how much interest you can earn with four different accounts.

item.title: Inside the Obama Presidential Center ahead of its star-studded opening
item.description: The Obama Presidential Center in Chicago strives to serve the public while honoring the legacy of America's 44th president. The center's star-studded grand opening on Juneteenth will feature musical guests such as John Legend, Bruce Springsteen and Christina Aguilera. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns takes a look inside.

item.title: Supreme Court rules against government in gun case surrounding drug use
item.description: The Supreme Court sided with a Texas man and ruled against the government after its prosecution of a federal law that bars certain drug users from having firearms. CBS News' Jan Crawford reports.

item.title: Tornadoes tear through multiple states across Midwest, Southeast
item.description: Tornadoes were reported in Illinois, Iowa and several other states Wednesday as severe weather slammed a large swath of the Midwest​ and Southeast.

item.title: Intel shares leap after Trump announces chip deal with Apple
item.description: In a social media post, President Trump touted the U.S. government's 10% stake in Intel, noting that it is now worth $60 billion.

item.title: Myles Smith talks debut album, "My Mess, My Heart, My Life," and touring with Ed Sheeran
item.description: Myles Smith's career skyrocketed after his hit song "Stargazing" became the biggest song by a U.K. artist worldwide in 2024. Now, he's releasing his debut album, "My Mess, My Heart, My Life," on June 19. Myles Smith joins "CBS Mornings" to discuss his rapid rise to stardom, his first album and his experience touring with Ed Sheeran.

item.title: Almost half of Americans think AI's future impact will be negative, Pew survey shows
item.description: A Pew survey shows 40% of Americans think AI's future impact will be negative. MIT Sloan professor Eric So joins CBS News with more details.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: Why didn't Clayton, Trump's pick for director of national intelligence, testify?
item.description: President Trump announced Wednesday that federal prosecutor Jay Clayton would not be testifying before a Senate panel of lawmakers for his confirmation process to be the director of national intelligence. CBS News' Taurean Small reports.

item.title: Gas prices fall below $4 a gallon for the first time in nearly 3 months
item.description: The national average for a gallon of regular gas in the U.S. drops to $3.99, the lowest since March 30.

item.title: Tornadoes ripped through cities; Tropical Storm Arthur floods parts of Texas and Louisiana
item.description: Severe weather slammed parts of the Midwest and the South. Tropical Storm Arthur flooded parts of Texas and Louisiana. CBS News' Jason Allen reports.

item.title: 1,200-year-old Robin Hood oak tree in Sherwood Forest has died, group says
item.description: A massive ancient oak tree linked to the legend of Robin Hood may have been loved to death.

item.title: Arrests of immigrant parents create mental health crisis for children
item.description: An estimated hundreds of thousands of children, many of them U.S. citizens, have been separated from a parent in the Trump administration's immigration crackdown.

item.title: Federal Reserve holds interest rates steady but leaves door open to hike
item.description: U.S. inflation is expected to remain elevated through the end of the year, Fed officials say in their latest forecast.

item.title: $50,000 CD vs. $50,000 high-yield savings account: Here's which can earn more over the next year
item.description: The interest earned from a $50,000 deposit in each account type will be similar but not identical. Here's what to know.

item.title: Letlow, Fleming advance to runoff in Louisiana GOP Senate primary, CBS News projects
item.description: Incumbent GOP Sen. Bill Cassidy, who had occasionally broken with the Trump administration, lost his bid for a third term.
